binaries.title: vhdmp.sys - Kernel-pointer information disclosure in the storage-dependency query handler (sub_1C0082CCC)

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0082CCC
key_changes: Patched version introduces a new gate call at function entry: `int32_t rax = sub_1c0010298();` (assembly: `call 0x1c0010298; mov dword [rsp+0xb0], eax`), whose result (rdx_2) is combined with the request flag `*(arg2+0x40)` to decide whether to disclose pointers.

Every place the pre-patch code stored a kernel object pointer into a caller output record now conditionally zeroes it. Pre-patch: `mov qword [r14+0x28], r13` (writes i_5, the dependent VHD-chain object). Post-patch: `mov rax, r15; cmp byte [rbp+0x40], dil; cmovne rax, rdi; mov qword [r14+0x28], rax` (NULs the pointer when the trust/flag check indicates an untrusted caller).

Same NUL-on-untrusted treatment applied to the dependency object pointer `i_5[0x85]`/`[r13+0x428]` stored at record+0x30, and to the per-iteration records in both the count==2 loop and the general loop (fields record+0x20 and +0x28).

Telemetry/ETW event codes shifted (0x1d -> 0x1f at exit; new 0x1b/0x1c/0x1d/0x1e event emissions) and the event data pointer changed from &data_1c00544a0 to &data_1c0052c78 — consistent with added logging around the sanitized path; not itself the vulnerability.
Control flow otherwise preserved: level validation (r9_1-1>2 => STATUS_INVALID_LEVEL), buffer-size computation (rbp_3/r13_3), and STATUS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW/BUFFER_TOO_SMALL handling are unchanged.
changed_functions.security_notes: The pre-patch handler copies raw kernel-mode object pointers (the dependent-chain object i_5 and its dependency object at i_5[0x428]/i_5[0x85]) directly into the STORAGE_DEPENDENCY_INFO records that are returned to the calling process. Because the output buffer is attacker-visible user memory, these fields disclose live kernel heap addresses, defeating KASLR and giving an attacker the exact base of internal vhdmp objects to target in a follow-on write/UAF primitive. The patch adds a caller-trust gate (sub_1c0010298) that, together with the request flag *(arg2+0x40), replaces those pointers with NULL (cmovne rax, rdi) for untrusted callers.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED (name_a=sub_1C0082CCC @ 0x1c0082ccc), '-' lines are the vulnerable pre-patch code; leak stores highlighted:
mov     qword [rsp+0x8], rbx
mov     qword [rsp+0x10], rdx
push    rbp / push rsi / push rdi / push r12 / push r13 / push r14 / push r15
sub     rsp, 0x40
mov     r12, rdx            ; r12 = arg2 (request)
mov     rbx, rcx            ; rbx = arg1
mov     rcx, qword [rel 0x1c005f048]
lea     rbp, [rel 0x1c005f048]
lea     r14, [rel 0x1c00544a0]   ; ETW event data (pre-patch)
cmp     rcx, rbp / je 0x1c0082d23
mov     rax, qword [r12+0xb8]     ; output buffer descriptor
mov     qword [rsp+0x90], rax
cmp     dword [rax+0x10], 0x4     ; level/version >= 4 gate
jae     0x1c0082d6d
... mov r15, qword [r12+0x18]      ; r15 = output buffer ptr
mov     r9d, dword [r15]          ; r9d = query level (1..3)
lea     eax, [r9-0x1] / cmp eax, 0x2 / ja 0x1c0083275   ; level>3 => STATUS_INVALID_LEVEL
... size/count computation building rbp_3 (required size) ...
cmp     eax, r9d / jb 0x1c0083245                       ; rax_12 (buf size) < required
mov     eax, ebp / mov qword [r14+0x8], rax             ; STATUS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW path
; ---- VULNERABLE POINTER STORES (pre-patch) ----
mov     qword [r14+0x28], r13     ; *(record+0x28) = i_5  (KERNEL POINTER LEAK)  @~0x1c0083127
cmp     dword [r15], 0x3 / jne 0x1c0083156
mov     rax, qword [r13+0x428]    ; dependency object
... mov qword [r15+0x28], rax     ; *(record+0x28) = i_5[0x85] (KERNEL POINTER LEAK) @~0x1c008314d
mov     qword [r15+0x20], r13     ; *(record+0x20) = dependent object (KERNEL POINTER LEAK) @~0x1c008319b
mov     rax, qword [r13+0x428] / mov qword [r15+0x28], rax   ; @~0x1c00831a5/0x1c00831c1
... records advanced by 0x30/0x40; loop over i_5 = i_5[0x92] ...
mov     qword [r12+0x38], rdi     ; *(arg2+0x38) = bytes written
; ---- exit / ETW ----
lea     r14, [rel 0x1c00544a0]
...
PATCHED counterpart replaces the entry with `call 0x1c0010298; mov dword [rsp+0xb0], eax` and each store becomes `cmp byte [rbp+0x40], dil; cmovne rax, rdi; mov qword [record], rax` (NUL for untrusted callers), plus new ETW codes 0x1b/0x1c/0x1d/0x1e and exit code 0x1f. (Full unified assembly diff captured via diff_function_diff forms=[assembly]; the per-binary disassembly worker was unreachable during analysis so offsets are taken from the diff hunks.)

security_findings.entry_point: DeviceIoControl / VirtDisk!GetStorageDependencyInformation path into vhdmp.sys (storage-dependency query IOCTL, e.g. IOCTL_STORAGE_GET_DEPENDENT_DISKS / the FSCTL used by GetStorageDependencyInformation) issued against a \\.\... VHD or dependent-volume handle
call_chain: user: OpenVirtualDisk / CreateFile on VHD-backed or dependent volume handle
vhdmp!sub_1C0082CCC (query-dependency-info handler, walks dependency chain and fills user output buffer)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Kernel-mode pointer information disclosure (CWE-200 / CWE-497: exposure of kernel addresses to unauthorized actor)
security_findings.description: The handler enumerates the VHD dependency chain (i_5 = current object, i_5[0x92] = next, i_5[0x85]/i_5[0x428] = dependency objects) and writes one fixed-size record per dependency into the caller-supplied output buffer at *(arg2+0x18). In the unpatched build these records include raw kernel-mode object pointers: the assembly `mov qword [r14+0x28], r13` stores the dependent-chain object pointer i_5, and `mov rax, qword [r13+0x428]; mov qword [r15+0x28], rax` stores the dependency object pointer, into fields that are copied back to user mode. No previous-mode/trust check gates the disclosure. The patched build calls sub_1c0010298() (a caller-trust/mode gate) and, when the request flag *(arg2+0x40) indicates an untrusted caller, replaces each pointer with NULL via `cmovne rax, rdi` before storing it.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Open a handle to a VHD-backed / dependent storage object and issue the storage-dependency query with: query level/version in {2,3} (first dword of *(arg2+0x18); level 1..3 valid, else STATUS_INVALID_LEVEL); output-buffer descriptor level *(arg2+0xb8)+0x10 >= 4; and an output buffer *(arg2+0x18) whose size *(arg2+0xb8)+8 is >= the computed required size (rbp_3) so the fill path (not the STATUS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW/TOO_SMALL early-outs) is taken. At least one dependency must exist in the chain so a record is emitted. On return, read the pointer field at offset +0x28 (and +0x20 for chained entries) of each returned record to recover the kernel address.
security_findings.primitive: Arbitrary kernel-heap address disclosure to a low-privileged/user-mode caller: leaks the base addresses of internal vhdmp dependency-chain objects and their sub-objects, defeating kernel ASLR and providing precise targets for a subsequent memory-corruption / UAF exploit chain.
security_findings.evidence: Unpatched pseudo-C (sub_1C0082CCC): `*(r14_1 + 0x28) = i_5;` then `... rax_25 = i_5[0x85]; ... *(r14_1 + 0x30) = rax_25;` and in the chain loop `*(r14_2 + 0x20) = rbx_6; ... rax_28 = rbx_6[0x85]; *(r14_2 + 0x28) = rax_28;` and count==2 branch `*(r15_2 + 0x20) = i_5; ... rax_14 = i_5[0x85]; *(r15_2 + 0x28) = rax_14;`. In the patched version each becomes gated: `int64_t* i_8 = i_5; if (rdx_2 && *(arg2 + 0x40)) i_8 = nullptr; *(r14_2 + 0x28) = i_8;` where `rdx_2 = rax = sub_1c0010298();`. These pointer-bearing records are the STORAGE_DEPENDENCY_INFO entries copied back to the caller.
security_findings.assembly:
Unpatched leak stores (from diff_function_diff forms=[assembly], '-' side = pre-patch; per-binary disasm worker was unreachable so exact VAs are the diff-hunk offsets):
mov     qword [r14+0x28], r13            ; @~0x1c0083127  *(record+0x28) = i_5  (kernel ptr)
cmp     dword [r15], 0x3
jne     0x1c0083156
mov     rax, qword [r13+0x428]
test    rcx, rcx / je 0x1c0083156
...
mov     qword [r15+0x28], rax            ; @~0x1c008314d  *(record+0x28) = i_5[0x85] (kernel ptr)
mov     qword [r15+0x20], r13            ; @~0x1c008319b  *(record+0x20) = dependent object (kernel ptr)
mov     rax, qword [r13+0x428]
mov     qword [r15+0x28], rax            ; @~0x1c00831a5/0x1c00831c1 dependency object (kernel ptr)
mov     qword [r15+0x20], r13            ; count==2 branch: *(record+0x20)=i_5
mov     rax, qword [r13+0x428] / mov qword [r15+0x28], rax
mov     qword [r12+0x38], rdi            ; *(arg2+0x38) = bytes written
Patched counterpart: `call 0x1c0010298; mov dword [rsp+0xb0], eax` at entry and for each store `cmp byte [rbp+0x40], dil; cmovne rax, rdi; mov qword [record], rax` (NUL for untrusted callers).

breakpoints.why: Function entry. rcx (arg1) = internal context/device object; rdx (arg2) = request object. Inspect *(rdx+0x18) (output buffer ptr, first dword = query level 1..3), *(rdx+0xb8) (output descriptor: +8 = buffer size, +0x10 = descriptor level), *(rdx+0x38) (bytes-written out), *(rdx+0x40) (request flag that the patch consults). This is where the patched build inserts `call 0x1c0010298`.

Pre-patch `mov qword [r14+0x28], r13`: r13 holds i_5, the live kernel pointer to the dependent VHD-chain object, being written into the output record. Dump r13 and the record at r14 here — that value is what leaks to user mode. In the patched binary this store is preceded by cmovne rax,rdi and writes 0 for untrusted callers.

Pre-patch store of the dependency object pointer i_5[0x85] (from [r13+0x428] after DependentFSReferenceDependency succeeds). Watch rax/[r13+0x428]; this kernel pointer is copied into the returned record.

Pre-patch `*(r14_2+0x20) = rbx_6` inside the multi-dependency loop: rbx_6 is the next chain object (kernel pointer) written into each subsequent output record. Confirms the leak scales to every dependency in the chain.

watch: rdx/rbp = arg2 (request object); *(rbp+0x40) byte = the flag the patch ANDs with the trust-check result
*(arg2+0x18) first dword = query level (set to 2 or 3 to reach the pointer-storing branches; 1 also stores i_5)
*(arg2+0xb8)+8 = output buffer size (must be >= computed required size rbp_3/r13_3 to take the fill path)
r13 (=i_5) and [r13+0x428] (=dependency object) at the store sites — these register values are the leaked kernel addresses
r14/r15 = current output record pointer; offsets +0x20, +0x28, +0x30 within the record receive the pointers
In the patched binary: eax result of call 0x1c0010298 saved at [rsp+0xb0]; the cmovne that zeroes the pointer
key_offsets: call 0x1c0010298 @ patched entry (mov dword [rsp+0xb0], eax) — the ADDED trust gate; absent in unpatched
mov qword [r14+0x28], r13 @ ~0x1c0083127 — pre-patch kernel-pointer store (i_5) with no sanitization
mov qword [r15+0x28], rax @ ~0x1c008314d / ~0x1c00831c1 — pre-patch dependency-object pointer store
mov qword [r15+0x20], r13 @ ~0x1c008319b — pre-patch chained-record pointer store
lea eax,[r9-1]; cmp eax,0x2; ja 0x1c0083275 — level validation (1..3 valid) at ~0x1c0082d80
cmp eax, r9d; jb 0x1c0083245 — output-buffer size check vs required size (fill vs BUFFER_OVERFLOW)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: From user mode: obtain a handle to a VHD-backed volume/disk that has at least one storage dependency (e.g. a mounted/attached VHD with a parent or a differencing chain), then call VirtDisk!GetStorageDependencyInformation (or issue the equivalent storage-dependency query DeviceIoControl) requesting STORAGE_DEPENDENCY_INFO version 2, with a large-enough output buffer to pass the size check. Break on vhdmp!sub_1C0082CCC (0x1c0082ccc) to confirm arg2 fields, then step to the leak stores.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: After the call returns STATUS_SUCCESS, the returned STORAGE_DEPENDENCY_INFO record(s) contain non-zero 64-bit values at the pointer offsets (record+0x20 / +0x28 / +0x30) that fall in the kernel address range (0xFFFF...); on the patched driver those same fields read as 0 for the same unprivileged caller. In WinDbg, `!pool` on the leaked value resolves to a vhdmp-tagged nonpaged pool object, confirming a real kernel object address was disclosed.
debugging_aids.notes: The per-binary disassembly worker (172.29.3.3:7332) was unreachable during analysis, so exact runtime VAs should be re-confirmed with `uf vhdmp!sub_1C0082CCC`; the offsets above come from the diff hunks and are stable relative to the function base 0x1c0082ccc. sub_1c0010298 (the added gate) takes no args and returns the int32 stored at [rsp+0xb0]; treat it as the caller-trust/previous-mode check — inspect it once the worker is back to confirm whether it keys on Irp->RequestorMode / ExGetPreviousMode or a global policy flag. Distinguish the vulnerable path from the safe one by whether the cmovne/NUL sequence precedes the pointer store (patched) or the pointer is stored directly (unpatched).

summary: Between these two builds of vhdmp.sys exactly one function changed: sub_1C0082CCC, the kernel handler that answers storage-dependency queries (GetStorageDependencyInformation) by walking the VHD dependency chain and filling a user-supplied output buffer with per-dependency records. The pre-patch code writes raw kernel-mode object pointers (the dependent-chain object i_5 and its dependency object at i_5[0x428]/i_5[0x85]) directly into those records at offsets +0x20/+0x28/+0x30, disclosing kernel heap addresses to any caller and defeating KASLR. The patch adds a caller-trust gate (sub_1c0010298) and, together with the request flag *(arg2+0x40), replaces each disclosed pointer with NULL (cmovne rax, rdi) for untrusted callers. A PoC needs only to open a VHD-backed/dependent-volume handle, request storage-dependency info (version 2/3) with a large-enough output buffer to reach the fill path, and read the kernel pointers from the returned records.
